name: Virgen del Campo Hermitage Trail
name_original: Ermita de Ntra. Sra. del Campo — ida y vuelta desde Aliseda
description: This moderate 6.2-mile jogging route to the Hermitage of Our Lady of the Field offers scenic views and historical points near Aliseda.
region_intro: Jogging around Aliseda, located in the province of Cáceres, offers routes through a landscape characterized by the northern slope of the Sierra del Aljibe and the broader Sierra de San Pedro. The region features riverine ecosystems, particularly along the Ribera and Salor rivers, with varied vegetation including willows, pines, and holm oaks. Trails often pass through areas with historical significance and provide views of the surrounding natural environment. Best jogging routes around Aliseda The most popular jogging route is Tumbas de los Moros loop from Aliseda, an easy 3.1 miles (4.9 km) trail that takes 31 minutes to complete. This path features gentle elevation changes through the local terrain. Another top favourite among local runners is Virgen del Campo Hermitage Trail, a moderate 6.2 miles (10.0 km) path. This route includes a notable elevation gain of 270 meters, offering a more challenging experience through varied landscapes. Local runners also love the Pozo loop from Ermita de Nuestra Señora de la Luz, a 7.2 miles (11.6 km) trail leading through the local countryside, often completed in about 1 hour 14 minutes. Jogging around Aliseda is defined by riverine paths, rolling hills, and diverse woodlands. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops to more challenging routes with significant elevation. The routes in Aliseda are highly rated by the komoot community. More than 40 runners have used komoot to explore Aliseda's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Aliseda?
A: There are 9 dedicated running routes around Aliseda listed on komoot, offering a variety of experiences for joggers.

Q: Are there any easy running routes for beginners in Aliseda?
A: Yes, Aliseda offers routes suitable for beginners. An excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31448104" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tumbas de los Moros loop from Aliseda&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 4.9 km trail with gentle elevation changes, typically completed in about 31 minutes.

Q: What are some challenging running trails in Aliseda?
A: For a more challenging experience, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1345944183/virgen-del-campo-hermitage-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Virgen del Campo Hermitage Trail&lt;/a&gt;. This moderate 10 km path includes a notable elevation gain of 270 meters, offering a more demanding run through varied landscapes.

Q: Are there circular running routes in Aliseda?
A: Many of the running paths in Aliseda are circular, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. Examples include the easy &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31448104" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tumbas de los Moros loop from Aliseda&lt;/a&gt; and the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31876397" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozo loop from Ermita de Nuestra Señora de la Luz&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on the jogging trails around Aliseda?
A: The jogging trails around Aliseda offer diverse scenery, characterized by riverine ecosystems along the Ribera and Salor rivers, rolling hills, and woodlands with willows, pines, and holm oaks. You can also expect views of the Sierra del Aljibe and the broader Sierra de San Pedro.

Q: Are there any historical or natural landmarks to see along the running routes?
A: Yes, the region has historical significance and natural beauty. The 'Ruta de las Fuentes' (Route of the Fountains) in Aliseda, for instance, guides you past an 18th-century flour mill and the Fuente del Cura. You might also encounter natural features like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5579441" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marmites of the Salor River&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5411654" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aceitunilla Pond&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Aliseda?
A: The running routes in Aliseda are highly regarded by the komoot community. Over 40 runners have explored the varied terrain, often praising the peaceful riverine paths and diverse woodlands.

Q: Are the running routes in Aliseda suitable for families?
A: Many routes in Aliseda, especially the easier, shorter loops, can be suitable for families looking for a gentle outdoor activity. The varied terrain offers options for different ability levels, allowing families to choose paths that match their fitness.

Q: What is the best season for jogging in Aliseda?
A: Aliseda, located in Extremadura, experiences warm summers. Spring and autumn generally offer more pleasant temperatures for jogging. Winter can also be suitable, with cooler but often mild weather, making it a good time to explore the trails without intense heat.

Q: Are there any long-distance running options in Aliseda?
A: While many routes are shorter to moderate, you can find longer options like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31876397" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozo loop from Ermita de Nuestra Señora de la Luz&lt;/a&gt;, which is 11.6 km. For even longer runs, you could combine multiple routes or explore the broader network of trails in the Cáceres province.

Q: Can I find running routes with significant elevation gain in Aliseda?
A: Yes, some routes offer significant elevation gain for those seeking a more intense workout. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1345944183/virgen-del-campo-hermitage-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Virgen del Campo Hermitage Trail&lt;/a&gt;, for example, features an elevation gain of 270 meters over its 10 km distance.
tips:
This 15th century church has stonework reinforcements and buttresses and is located in the heart of the town.
The nave has three rectangular sections.

This was an old iron mine that was exploited in the early and mid-20th century.
Currently, there is an interpretation center that allows you to visit it and learn about its history and iron extraction. Visiting hours are at 10 a.m. and 12 p.m. and you can go from Tuesday to Sunday. To visit it, you must call 606 306 281 in advance.
You can find more information at: https://www.aliseda.es/mina-pastora

This beautiful hermitage with its characteristic white whitewashed masonry is dedicated to the patron saint of the town of Aliseda.
The hermitage is a popular work from the 17th century, although a century later it underwent new modifications.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Virgen del Campo Hermitage Trail?
faqs.answer: While specific parking details aren't provided, trails leading to hermitages often have designated parking areas near the trailhead, especially if they are popular. It's advisable to check local maps or komoot's route planner for the most precise starting point and parking options for this specific route.

faqs.question: Is the Virgen del Campo Hermitage Trail suitable for beginner joggers?
faqs.answer: The trail is rated as moderate for jogging, with an elevation gain of approximately 270 meters over nearly 10 kilometers. This suggests it's best suited for joggers with a good level of fitness. Beginners might find some sections challenging due to the elevation changes.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain on trails like this can be quite varied. You might encounter a mix of dirt roads and hiking paths. Given its moderate difficulty, expect some uneven surfaces and potentially rocky sections, especially on ascents and descents. Sturdy jogging shoes are recommended.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you'll encounter several notable highlights. These include the historic Church of Our Lady of the Assumption, the intriguing La Pastora Mine, and the spiritual Hermitage of Our Lady of the Field itself.

faqs.question: Does the trail connect with other paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several local paths. You'll be jogging along parts of the Cordel Camino De la Ermita De La Virgen Del Campo, Calle Puerto, and Subida Pericuto, offering a varied experience of the local trail network.

faqs.question: Is the Virgen del Campo Hermitage Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While many trails in natural areas are dog-friendly, it's always best to keep your dog on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure the comfort of other trail users. Always clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required to jog in Sierra de San Pedro?
faqs.answer: Generally, trails in natural areas like Sierra de San Pedro do not require specific permits or entrance fees for individual use. However, it's always a good idea to check for any local regulations or protected area rules before your visit, especially if you plan to visit during specific events or with a large group.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this trail?
faqs.answer: The best time to jog this trail is typically during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Summer can be very hot, making midday jogging challenging, while winter might bring colder temperatures and potentially wet conditions, depending on the specific weather patterns in Sierra de San Pedro.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to jog the Virgen del Campo Hermitage Trail?
faqs.answer: For an average jogger, this 10-kilometer route with moderate elevation gain typically takes around 1 hour and 7 minutes to complete. This duration can vary based on your fitness level and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: What kind of views can I expect while jogging?
faqs.answer: The trail offers picturesque and varied scenery. You can expect panoramic views of the surrounding natural landscape, which often includes green pastures, forests, and distant vistas. The elevated points, especially near the hermitage, typically provide excellent viewpoints.

faqs.question: Is the Virgen del Campo Hermitage Trail a loop?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Virgen del Campo Hermitage Trail is designed as a round trip, meaning it's a loop trail that brings you back to your starting point. This makes it convenient for planning your outing.
